title: Materials and methods for enhancing physical properties, dynamic properties and dispersion in carbon black and mixtures containing carbon black
abstract: The present invention provides materials and methods to enhance dispersion of carbon black within polymer systems, improving both static physical properties and dynamic physical properties of polymers (such as natural or synthetic rubber) containing carbon black. Some variations provide a composition containing a polymer, carbon black, and a performance-enhancing additive comprising amorphous silica, wherein the performance-enhancing additive causes a property improvement associated with or imparted by the carbon black. The performance-enhancing additive scrubs or exfoliates the carbon black in situ, to improve tensile strength, modulus, tear, elongation, dispersion, flow properties, viscosity, hardness, specific gravity, rebound, torque, and/or load of the polymer composite.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A composition containing a polymer, carbon black, and a performance- enhancing additive comprising amorphous silica, wherein said performance- enhancing additive causes a property improvement associated with or imparted by said carbon black, compared to an otherwise-identical composition that does not contain said performance-enhancing additive, wherein said property is selected from static physical properties, dynamic physical properties, dispersion, and combinations thereof. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein said performance-enhancing additive scrubs or exfoliates said carbon black in situ. 3. The composition of claim 1, wherein said amorphous silica is present in said composition at a concentration from about 0.1 wt% to about 10 wt%. 4. The composition of claim 3, wherein said amorphous silica is present in said composition at a concentration from about 0.5 wt% to about 5 wt%. 5. The composition of claim 1, wherein said amorphous silica is derived from a material selected from the group consisting of pumice, perlite, another natural volcanic material with a similar morphology to pumice or perlite, and combinations thereof. 6. The composition of claim 1, wherein said performance-enhancing additive is derived from an irregularly shaped, porous material. 7. The composition of claim 6, wherein said irregularly shaped, porous material is a synthetic ceramic material. 8. The composition of claim 5, wherein said amorphous silica is present as finely ground pumice. 9. The composition of claim 8, wherein said performance-enhancing additive consists essentially of said finely ground pumice. 10. The composition of claim 1, wherein said performance-enhancing additive is characterized by an average particle size selected from about 1 micron to about 10 microns. 11. The composition of claim 10, wherein said average particle size is selected from about 3 microns to about 7 microns. 12. The composition of claim 1, wherein said performance-enhancing additive contains less than 1 wt% of particles that are less than 1 micron in particle size. 13. The composition of claim 1, said performance-enhancing additive further comprising one or more materials selected from the group consisting of kaolin, talc, and mica. 14. The composition of claim 13, wherein said amorphous silica is present from about 50 wt% to about 99 wt% of said performance-enhancing additive. 15. The composition of claim 1, wherein said property is selected from the group consisting of tensile strength, modulus, tear, elongation, dispersion, flow properties, viscosity, hardness, specific gravity, rebound, torque, load, and combinations thereof. 16. The composition of claim 1, wherein said polymer is a natural or synthetic rubber. 17. A method of enhancing physical properties, dynamic properties, and/or dispersion in a material containing a polymer and carbon black, said method comprising mixing a performance-enhancing additive comprising amorphous silica into said material, wherein said performance-enhancing additive causes a property improvement associated with or imparted by said carbon black, compared to an otherwise-identical composition that does not contain said performance-enhancing additive, wherein said property is selected from static physical properties, dynamic physical properties, dispersion, and combinations thereof. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein said performance-enhancing additive scrubs or exfoliates said carbon black in situ. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein said property is selected from the group consisting of tensile strength, modulus, tear, elongation, dispersion, flow properties, viscosity, hardness, specific gravity, rebound, torque, load, and combinations thereof. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein said polymer is a natural or synthetic rubber. 21. The method of claim 17, wherein said performance-enhancing additive is added to said carbon black prior to dispersion of a mixture of said carbon black and said performance-enhancing additive into said polymer. 22. The method of claim 17, wherein said performance-enhancing additive is added directly to said polymer, separately from dispersion of said carbon black into said polymer. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein said carbon black is added to said polymer, and then said performance-enhancing additive is added to said polymer. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein said performance-enhancing additive is added to said polymer, and then said carbon black is added to said polymer. 25. The method of claim 17, wherein said amorphous silica is present as finely ground pumice. 26. The method of claim 17, wherein said performance-enhancing additive is characterized by an average particle size selected from about 1 micron to about 10 microns.
